title: Greetings and introductions
summary: Choose formal or informal greetings and use the first introduction phrases from the original course.

sections.heading: Formal situations
sections.body: Use formal greetings with instructors, strangers, staff in shops or offices, police officers, waiters, and adults you do not know personally.

sections.heading: Informal situations
sections.body: Ahoj, čau, and nazdar are used mainly with friends, fellow students, and children. Ahoj and čau can also mean goodbye.

sections.heading: Introductions
sections.body: Use Já jsem… to say who you are and To je… to introduce another person. Těší mě is a conventional response when meeting someone.
examples: Těší mě. — Pleased to meet you.

resources.title: Dobrý den!
resources.note: Authentic examples of the formal greeting.

exercises.prompt: You enter an office and address an adult you do not know. What is the safest greeting?
exercises.explanation: Dobrý den is the standard formal or neutral greeting.
Na shledanou is the full form; nashle is a common shortened form.

exercises.prompt: Which phrase means “Pleased to meet you”?
exercises.explanation: Těší mě is the conventional phrase.
